name: anyio

metadata.name: anyio
metadata.summary: High level compatibility layer for multiple asynchronous event loop implementations

documentation:
```{image} https://github.com/agronholm/anyio/actions/workflows/test.yml/badge.svg
:alt: Build Status
:target: https://github.com/agronholm/anyio/actions/workflows/test.yml
```

```{image} https://coveralls.io/repos/github/agronholm/anyio/badge.svg?branch=master
:alt: Code Coverage
:target: https://coveralls.io/github/agronholm/anyio?branch=master
```

```{image} https://readthedocs.org/projects/anyio/badge/?version=latest
:alt: Documentation
:target: https://anyio.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest
```

```{image} https://badges.gitter.im/gitterHQ/gitter.svg
:alt: Gitter chat
:target: https://gitter.im/python-trio/AnyIO
```

AnyIO is an asynchronous networking and concurrency library that works on top of either [asyncio] or
[trio]. It implements trio-like [structured concurrency] (SC) on top of asyncio, and works in harmony
with the native SC of trio itself.

Applications and libraries written against AnyIO's API will run unmodified on either [asyncio] or
[trio]. AnyIO can also be adopted into a library or application incrementally – bit by bit, no full
refactoring necessary. It will blend in with native libraries of your chosen backend.

# Documentation

View full documentation at: <https://anyio.readthedocs.io/>

# Features

AnyIO offers the following functionality:

- Task groups ([nurseries] in trio terminology)

- High level networking (TCP, UDP and UNIX sockets)

  - [Happy eyeballs] algorithm for TCP connections (more robust than that of asyncio on Python
    3.8)
  - async/await style UDP sockets (unlike asyncio where you still have to use Transports and
    Protocols)

- A versatile API for byte streams and object streams

- Inter-task synchronization and communication (locks, conditions, events, semaphores, object
  streams)

- Worker threads

- Subprocesses

- Asynchronous file I/O (using worker threads)

- Signal handling

AnyIO also comes with its own [pytest] plugin which also supports asynchronous fixtures.
It even works with the popular [Hypothesis] library.

[asyncio]: https://docs.python.org/3/library/asyncio.html
[happy eyeballs]: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Happy_Eyeballs
[hypothesis]: https://hypothesis.works/
[nurseries]: https://trio.readthedocs.io/en/stable/reference-core.html#nurseries-and-spawning
[pytest]: https://docs.pytest.org/en/latest/
[structured concurrency]: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Structured_concurrency
[trio]: https://github.com/python-trio/trio

classes.name: anyio.abc._resources.AsyncResource

methods.name: anyio.abc._resources.AsyncResource.aclose
documentation: Close the resource.

Abstract base class for all closeable asynchronous resources.

Works as an asynchronous context manager which returns the instance itself on enter, and calls
{meth}`aclose` on exit.

classes.name: anyio.abc._subprocesses.Process

methods.name: anyio.abc._subprocesses.Process.wait
documentation:
Wait until the process exits.

:return: the exit code of the process

methods.name: anyio.abc._subprocesses.Process.terminate
documentation:
Terminates the process, gracefully if possible.

On Windows, this calls `TerminateProcess()`.
On POSIX systems, this sends `SIGTERM` to the process.

:::{seealso}
{meth}`subprocess.Popen.terminate`
:::

methods.name: anyio.abc._subprocesses.Process.kill
documentation:
Kills the process.

On Windows, this calls `TerminateProcess()`.
On POSIX systems, this sends `SIGKILL` to the process.

:::{seealso}
{meth}`subprocess.Popen.kill`
:::

methods.name: anyio.abc._subprocesses.Process.send_signal
documentation:
Send a signal to the subprocess.

:::{seealso}
{meth}`subprocess.Popen.send_signal`
:::

```{eval-rst}

:param signal: the signal number (e.g. :data:`signal.SIGHUP`)
```

methods.name: anyio.abc._subprocesses.Process.pid
documentation: The process ID of the process.

methods.name: anyio.abc._subprocesses.Process.returncode
documentation:
The return code of the process. If the process has not yet terminated, this will be
`None`.

methods.name: anyio.abc._subprocesses.Process.stdin
documentation: The stream for the standard input of the process.

methods.name: anyio.abc._subprocesses.Process.stdout
documentation: The stream for the standard output of the process.

methods.name: anyio.abc._subprocesses.Process.stderr
documentation: The stream for the standard error output of the process.
An asynchronous version of {class}`subprocess.Popen`.

methods.name: anyio.abc._tasks.TaskStatus.started
documentation:
Signal that the task has started.

:param value: object passed back to the starter of the task

classes.name: anyio.abc._tasks.TaskGroup

methods.name: anyio.abc._tasks.TaskGroup.spawn
documentation:
Start a new task in this task group.

```{eval-rst}

:param func: a coroutine function
:param args: positional arguments to call the function with
:param name: name of the task, for the purposes of introspection and debugging
```

:::{deprecated} 3.0 Use :meth:`start_soon` instead. If your code needs AnyIO 2 compatibility, you can keep using this until AnyIO 4.
:::

methods.name: anyio.abc._tasks.TaskGroup.start_soon
documentation:
Start a new task in this task group.

```{eval-rst}

:param func: a coroutine function
:param args: positional arguments to call the function with
:param name: name of the task, for the purposes of introspection and debugging
```

:::{versionadded} 3.0
:::

methods.name: anyio.abc._tasks.TaskGroup.start
documentation:
Start a new task and wait until it signals for readiness.

```{eval-rst}

:param func: a coroutine function
:param args: positional arguments to call the function with
:param name: name of the task, for the purposes of introspection and debugging
:return: the value passed to ``task_status.started()``
:raises RuntimeError: if the task finishes without calling ``task_status.started()``
```

:::{versionadded} 3.0
:::

methods.name: anyio.abc._tasks.TaskGroup.__aenter__
documentation: Enter the task group context and allow starting new tasks.

methods.name: anyio.abc._tasks.TaskGroup.__aexit__
documentation: Exit the task group context waiting for all tasks to finish.

Groups several asynchronous tasks together.

:ivar cancel_scope: the cancel scope inherited by all child tasks
:vartype cancel_scope: CancelScope

classes.name: anyio.abc._sockets.SocketStream
documentation:
Transports bytes over a socket.

Supports all relevant extra attributes from {class}`~SocketAttribute`.

methods.name: anyio.abc._sockets.UNIXSocketStream.send_fds
documentation:
Send file descriptors along with a message to the peer.

:param message: a non-empty bytestring
:param fds: a collection of files (either numeric file descriptors or open file or socket
    objects)

methods.name: anyio.abc._sockets.UNIXSocketStream.receive_fds
documentation:
Receive file descriptors along with a message from the peer.

:param msglen: length of the message to expect from the peer
:param maxfds: maximum number of file descriptors to expect from the peer
:return: a tuple of (message, file descriptors)

classes.name: anyio.abc._sockets.SocketListener

methods.name: anyio.abc._sockets.SocketListener.accept
documentation: Accept an incoming connection.

Listens to incoming socket connections.

Supports all relevant extra attributes from {class}`~SocketAttribute`.

classes.name: anyio.abc._sockets.UDPSocket

methods.name: anyio.abc._sockets.UDPSocket.sendto
documentation: Alias for {meth}`~.UnreliableObjectSendStream.send` ((data, (host, port))).

Represents an unconnected UDP socket.

Supports all relevant extra attributes from {class}`~SocketAttribute`.

classes.name: anyio.abc._sockets.ConnectedUDPSocket
documentation:
Represents an connected UDP socket.

Supports all relevant extra attributes from {class}`~SocketAttribute`.

classes.name: anyio.abc._streams.UnreliableObjectReceiveStream

methods.name: anyio.abc._streams.UnreliableObjectReceiveStream.receive
documentation:
Receive the next item.

:raises ~anyio.ClosedResourceError: if the receive stream has been explicitly
    closed
:raises ~anyio.EndOfStream: if this stream has been closed from the other end
:raises ~anyio.BrokenResourceError: if this stream has been rendered unusable
    due to external causes

An interface for receiving objects.

This interface makes no guarantees that the received messages arrive in the order in which they
were sent, or that no messages are missed.

Asynchronously iterating over objects of this type will yield objects matching the given type
parameter.

classes.name: anyio.abc._streams.UnreliableObjectSendStream

methods.name: anyio.abc._streams.UnreliableObjectSendStream.send
documentation:
Send an item to the peer(s).

:param item: the item to send
:raises ~anyio.ClosedResourceError: if the send stream has been explicitly
    closed
:raises ~anyio.BrokenResourceError: if this stream has been rendered unusable
    due to external causes

An interface for sending objects.

This interface makes no guarantees that the messages sent will reach the recipient(s) in the
same order in which they were sent, or at all.

classes.name: anyio.abc._streams.UnreliableObjectStream
documentation:
A bidirectional message stream which does not guarantee the order or reliability of message
delivery.

classes.name: anyio.abc._streams.ObjectReceiveStream
documentation:
A receive message stream which guarantees that messages are received in the same order in
which they were sent, and that no messages are missed.

classes.name: anyio.abc._streams.ObjectSendStream
documentation:
A send message stream which guarantees that messages are delivered in the same order in which
they were sent, without missing any messages in the middle.

classes.name: anyio.abc._streams.ObjectStream

methods.name: anyio.abc._streams.ObjectStream.send_eof
documentation:
Send an end-of-file indication to the peer.

You should not try to send any further data to this stream after calling this method.
This method is idempotent (does nothing on successive calls).
A bidirectional message stream which guarantees the order and reliability of message delivery.

classes.name: anyio.abc._streams.ByteReceiveStream

methods.name: anyio.abc._streams.ByteReceiveStream.receive
documentation:
Receive at most `max_bytes` bytes from the peer.

:::{note}
Implementors of this interface should not return an empty {class}`bytes` object,
and users should ignore them.
:::

```{eval-rst}

:param max_bytes: maximum number of bytes to receive
:return: the received bytes
:raises ~anyio.EndOfStream: if this stream has been closed from the other end
```

An interface for receiving bytes from a single peer.

Iterating this byte stream will yield a byte string of arbitrary length, but no more than
65536 bytes.

classes.name: anyio.abc._streams.ByteSendStream

methods.name: anyio.abc._streams.ByteSendStream.send
documentation:
Send the given bytes to the peer.

:param item: the bytes to send
An interface for sending bytes to a single peer.

classes.name: anyio.abc._streams.ByteStream

methods.name: anyio.abc._streams.ByteStream.send_eof
documentation:
Send an end-of-file indication to the peer.

You should not try to send any further data to this stream after calling this method.
This method is idempotent (does nothing on successive calls).
A bidirectional byte stream.

classes.name: anyio.abc._streams.Listener

methods.name: anyio.abc._streams.Listener.serve
documentation:
Accept incoming connections as they come in and start tasks to handle them.

:param handler: a callable that will be used to handle each accepted connection
:param task_group: the task group that will be used to start tasks for handling each
    accepted connection (if omitted, an ad-hoc task group will be created)
An interface for objects that let you accept incoming connections.

classes.name: anyio.abc._testing.TestRunner

methods.name: anyio.abc._testing.TestRunner.close
documentation: Close the event loop.

methods.name: anyio.abc._testing.TestRunner.run_asyncgen_fixture
documentation:
Run an async generator fixture.

:param fixture_func: the fixture function
:param kwargs: keyword arguments to call the fixture function with
:return: an iterator yielding the value yielded from the async generator

methods.name: anyio.abc._testing.TestRunner.run_fixture
documentation:
Run an async fixture.

:param fixture_func: the fixture function
:param kwargs: keyword arguments to call the fixture function with
:return: the return value of the fixture function

methods.name: anyio.abc._testing.TestRunner.run_test
documentation:
Run an async test function.

:param test_func: the test function
:param kwargs: keyword arguments to call the test function with

Encapsulates a running event loop. Every call made through this object will use the same event
loop.

classes.name: anyio.streams.buffered.BufferedByteReceiveStream

methods.name: anyio.streams.buffered.BufferedByteReceiveStream.buffer
documentation: The bytes currently in the buffer.

methods.name: anyio.streams.buffered.BufferedByteReceiveStream.receive_exactly
documentation:
Read exactly the given amount of bytes from the stream.

:param nbytes: the number of bytes to read
:return: the bytes read
:raises ~anyio.IncompleteRead: if the stream was closed before the requested
    amount of bytes could be read from the stream

methods.name: anyio.streams.buffered.BufferedByteReceiveStream.receive_until
documentation:
Read from the stream until the delimiter is found or max_bytes have been read.

```{eval-rst}

:param delimiter: the marker to look for in the stream
:param max_bytes: maximum number of bytes that will be read before raising
    :exc:`~anyio.DelimiterNotFound`
:return: the bytes read (not including the delimiter)
:raises ~anyio.IncompleteRead: if the stream was closed before the delimiter
    was found
:raises ~anyio.DelimiterNotFound: if the delimiter is not found within the
    bytes read up to the maximum allowed
```

Wraps any bytes-based receive stream and uses a buffer to provide sophisticated receiving
capabilities in the form of a byte stream.

classes.name: anyio.streams.stapled.StapledByteStream
documentation:
Combines two byte streams into a single, bidirectional byte stream.

Extra attributes will be provided from both streams, with the receive stream providing the
values in case of a conflict.

:param ByteSendStream send_stream: the sending byte stream
:param ByteReceiveStream receive_stream: the receiving byte stream

classes.name: anyio.streams.stapled.StapledObjectStream
documentation:
Combines two object streams into a single, bidirectional object stream.

Extra attributes will be provided from both streams, with the receive stream providing the
values in case of a conflict.

:param ObjectSendStream send_stream: the sending object stream
:param ObjectReceiveStream receive_stream: the receiving object stream

classes.name: anyio.streams.stapled.MultiListener
documentation:
Combines multiple listeners into one, serving connections from all of them at once.

Any MultiListeners in the given collection of listeners will have their listeners moved into
this one.

Extra attributes are provided from each listener, with each successive listener overriding any
conflicting attributes from the previous one.

:param listeners: listeners to serve
:type listeners: Sequence[Listener[T_Stream]]

functions.name: anyio.lowlevel.checkpoint
documentation:
Check for cancellation and allow the scheduler to switch to another task.

Equivalent to (but more efficient than):

```
await checkpoint_if_cancelled()
await cancel_shielded_checkpoint()
```

:::{versionadded} 3.0
:::

functions.name: anyio.lowlevel.checkpoint_if_cancelled
documentation:
Enter a checkpoint if the enclosing cancel scope has been cancelled.

This does not allow the scheduler to switch to a different task.

:::{versionadded} 3.0
:::

functions.name: anyio.lowlevel.cancel_shielded_checkpoint
documentation:
Allow the scheduler to switch to another task but without checking for cancellation.

Equivalent to (but potentially more efficient than):

```
with CancelScope(shield=True):
    await checkpoint()
```

:::{versionadded} 3.0
:::

functions.name: anyio.lowlevel.current_token
documentation: Return a backend specific token object that can be used to get back to the event loop.

classes.name: anyio.lowlevel.RunVar
documentation: Like a {class}`~contextvars.ContextVar`, expect scoped to the running event loop.

classes.name: anyio.streams.file.FileReadStream

methods.name: anyio.streams.file.FileReadStream.from_path
documentation:
Create a file read stream by opening the given file.

:param path: path of the file to read from

methods.name: anyio.streams.file.FileReadStream.seek
documentation:
Seek the file to the given position.

:::{seealso}
{meth}`io.IOBase.seek`
:::

:::{note}
Not all file descriptors are seekable.
:::

```{eval-rst}

:param position: position to seek the file to
:param whence: controls how ``position`` is interpreted
:return: the new absolute position
:raises OSError: if the file is not seekable
```

methods.name: anyio.streams.file.FileReadStream.tell
documentation:
Return the current stream position.

:::{note}
Not all file descriptors are seekable.
:::

```{eval-rst}

:return: the current absolute position
:raises OSError: if the file is not seekable
```

A byte stream that reads from a file in the file system.

:::{versionadded} 3.0
:::

classes.name: anyio.streams.file.FileWriteStream

methods.name: anyio.streams.file.FileWriteStream.from_path
documentation:
Create a file write stream by opening the given file for writing.

```{eval-rst}

:param path: path of the file to write to
:param append: if ``True``, open the file for appending; if ``False``, any existing file
    at the given path will be truncated
```

A byte stream that writes to a file in the file system.

:::{versionadded} 3.0
:::

classes.name: anyio.streams.text.TextReceiveStream
documentation:
Stream wrapper that decodes bytes to strings using the given encoding.

Decoding is done using {class}`~codecs.IncrementalDecoder` which returns any completely
received unicode characters as soon as they come in.

```{eval-rst}

:param transport_stream: any bytes-based receive stream
:param encoding: character encoding to use for decoding bytes to strings (defaults to
    ``utf-8``)
:param errors: handling scheme for decoding errors (defaults to ``strict``; see the
    `codecs module documentation`_ for a comprehensive list of options)
```

[codecs module documentation]: https://docs.python.org/3/library/codecs.html#codec-objects

classes.name: anyio.streams.text.TextSendStream
documentation:
Sends strings to the wrapped stream as bytes using the given encoding.

```{eval-rst}

:param AnyByteSendStream transport_stream: any bytes-based send stream
:param str encoding: character encoding to use for encoding strings to bytes (defaults to
    ``utf-8``)
:param str errors: handling scheme for encoding errors (defaults to ``strict``; see the
    `codecs module documentation`_ for a comprehensive list of options)
```

[codecs module documentation]: https://docs.python.org/3/library/codecs.html#codec-objects

classes.name: anyio.streams.text.TextStream
documentation:
A bidirectional stream that decodes bytes to strings on receive and encodes strings to bytes on
send.

Extra attributes will be provided from both streams, with the receive stream providing the
values in case of a conflict.

```{eval-rst}

:param AnyByteStream transport_stream: any bytes-based stream
:param str encoding: character encoding to use for encoding/decoding strings to/from bytes
    (defaults to ``utf-8``)
:param str errors: handling scheme for encoding errors (defaults to ``strict``; see the
    `codecs module documentation`_ for a comprehensive list of options)
```

[codecs module documentation]: https://docs.python.org/3/library/codecs.html#codec-objects

methods.name: anyio.streams.memory.MemoryObjectReceiveStream.receive_nowait
documentation:
Receive the next item if it can be done without waiting.

:return: the received item
:raises ~anyio.ClosedResourceError: if this send stream has been closed
:raises ~anyio.EndOfStream: if the buffer is empty and this stream has been
    closed from the sending end
:raises ~anyio.WouldBlock: if there are no items in the buffer and no tasks
    waiting to send

methods.name: anyio.streams.memory.MemoryObjectReceiveStream.clone
documentation:
Create a clone of this receive stream.

Each clone can be closed separately. Only when all clones have been closed will the
receiving end of the memory stream be considered closed by the sending ends.

:return: the cloned stream

methods.name: anyio.streams.memory.MemoryObjectReceiveStream.close
documentation:
Close the stream.

This works the exact same way as {meth}`aclose`, but is provided as a special case for the
benefit of synchronous callbacks.

methods.name: anyio.streams.memory.MemoryObjectReceiveStream.statistics
documentation:
Return statistics about the current state of this stream.

:::{versionadded} 3.0
:::

methods.name: anyio.streams.memory.MemoryObjectSendStream.send_nowait
documentation:
Send an item immediately if it can be done without waiting.

:param item: the item to send
:raises ~anyio.ClosedResourceError: if this send stream has been closed
:raises ~anyio.BrokenResourceError: if the stream has been closed from the
    receiving end
:raises ~anyio.WouldBlock: if the buffer is full and there are no tasks waiting
    to receive

methods.name: anyio.streams.memory.MemoryObjectSendStream.clone
documentation:
Create a clone of this send stream.

Each clone can be closed separately. Only when all clones have been closed will the
sending end of the memory stream be considered closed by the receiving ends.

:return: the cloned stream

methods.name: anyio.streams.memory.MemoryObjectSendStream.close
documentation:
Close the stream.

This works the exact same way as {meth}`aclose`, but is provided as a special case for the
benefit of synchronous callbacks.

methods.name: anyio.streams.memory.MemoryObjectSendStream.statistics
documentation:
Return statistics about the current state of this stream.

:::{versionadded} 3.0
:::

functions.name: anyio._core._resources.aclose_forcefully
documentation:
Close an asynchronous resource in a cancelled scope.

Doing this closes the resource without waiting on anything.

:param resource: the resource to close

functions.name: anyio._core._signals.open_signal_receiver
documentation:
Start receiving operating system signals.

```{eval-rst}

:param signals: signals to receive (e.g. ``signal.SIGINT``)
:return: an asynchronous context manager for an asynchronous iterator which yields signal
    numbers
```

:::{warning}
Windows does not support signals natively so it is best to avoid relying on this
in cross-platform applications.
:::

:::{warning}
On asyncio, this permanently replaces any previous signal handler for the given
signals, as set via {meth}`~asyncio.loop.add_signal_handler`.
:::

functions.name: anyio.to_thread.run_sync
documentation:
Call the given function with the given arguments in a worker thread.

If the `cancellable` option is enabled and the task waiting for its completion is cancelled,
the thread will still run its course but its return value (or any raised exception) will be
ignored.

```{eval-rst}

:param func: a callable
:param args: positional arguments for the callable
:param cancellable: ``True`` to allow cancellation of the operation
:param limiter: capacity limiter to use to limit the total amount of threads running
    (if omitted, the default limiter is used)
:return: an awaitable that yields the return value of the function.
```

functions.name: anyio.to_thread.current_default_thread_limiter
documentation:
Return the capacity limiter that is used by default to limit the number of concurrent threads.

:return: a capacity limiter object

functions.name: anyio._core._streams.create_memory_object_stream
documentation:
Create a memory object stream.

```{eval-rst}

:param max_buffer_size: number of items held in the buffer until ``send()`` starts blocking
:param item_type: type of item, for marking the streams with the right generic type for
    static typing (not used at run time)
:return: a tuple of (send stream, receive stream)
```

classes.name: anyio._core._exceptions.BrokenResourceError
documentation:
Raised when trying to use a resource that has been rendered unusable due to external causes
(e.g. a send stream whose peer has disconnected).

classes.name: anyio._core._exceptions.BrokenWorkerProcess
documentation:
Raised by {func}`run_sync_in_process` if the worker process terminates abruptly or otherwise
misbehaves.

classes.name: anyio._core._exceptions.BusyResourceError
documentation: Raised when two tasks are trying to read from or write to the same resource concurrently.

classes.name: anyio._core._exceptions.ClosedResourceError
documentation: Raised when trying to use a resource that has been closed.

classes.name: anyio._core._exceptions.DelimiterNotFound
documentation:
Raised during {meth}`~anyio.streams.buffered.BufferedByteReceiveStream.receive_until` if the
maximum number of bytes has been read without the delimiter being found.

classes.name: anyio._core._exceptions.EndOfStream
documentation: Raised when trying to read from a stream that has been closed from the other end.

classes.name: anyio._core._exceptions.ExceptionGroup
documentation:
Raised when multiple exceptions have been raised in a task group.

:var ~typing.Sequence[BaseException] exceptions: the sequence of exceptions raised together

classes.name: anyio._core._exceptions.IncompleteRead
documentation:
Raised during {meth}`~anyio.streams.buffered.BufferedByteReceiveStream.receive_exactly` or
{meth}`~anyio.streams.buffered.BufferedByteReceiveStream.receive_until` if the
connection is closed before the requested amount of bytes has been read.

classes.name: anyio._core._exceptions.TypedAttributeLookupError
documentation:
Raised by {meth}`~anyio.TypedAttributeProvider.extra` when the given typed attribute is not
found and no default value has been given.

classes.name: anyio._core._exceptions.WouldBlock
documentation: Raised by `X_nowait` functions if `X()` would block.

functions.name: anyio._core._eventloop.run
documentation:
Run the given coroutine function in an asynchronous event loop.

The current thread must not be already running an event loop.

```{eval-rst}

:param func: a coroutine function
:param args: positional arguments to ``func``
:param backend: name of the asynchronous event loop implementation – currently either
    ``asyncio`` or ``trio``
:param backend_options: keyword arguments to call the backend ``run()`` implementation with
    (documented :ref:`here <backend options>`)
:return: the return value of the coroutine function
:raises RuntimeError: if an asynchronous event loop is already running in this thread
:raises LookupError: if the named backend is not found
```

functions.name: anyio._core._eventloop.sleep
documentation:
Pause the current task for the specified duration.

:param delay: the duration, in seconds

functions.name: anyio._core._eventloop.sleep_forever
documentation:
Pause the current task until it's cancelled.

This is a shortcut for `sleep(math.inf)`.

:::{versionadded} 3.1
:::

functions.name: anyio._core._eventloop.sleep_until
documentation:
Pause the current task until the given time.

```{eval-rst}

.. versionadded:: 3.1
```

:::{versionadded} 3.1
:::

functions.name: anyio._core._eventloop.current_time
documentation:
Return the current value of the event loop's internal clock.

:return: the clock value (seconds)

functions.name: anyio._core._eventloop.get_all_backends
documentation: Return a tuple of the names of all built-in backends.

functions.name: anyio._core._eventloop.get_cancelled_exc_class
documentation: Return the current async library's cancellation exception class.

functions.name: anyio._core._eventloop.claim_worker_thread
documentation: Private API

functions.name: anyio._core._testing.get_current_task
documentation:
Return the current task.

:return: a representation of the current task

functions.name: anyio._core._testing.get_running_tasks
documentation:
Return a list of running tasks in the current event loop.

:return: a list of task info objects

functions.name: anyio._core._testing.wait_all_tasks_blocked
documentation: Wait until all other tasks are waiting for something.

classes.name: anyio._core._testing.TaskInfo
documentation:
Represents an asynchronous task.

:ivar int id: the unique identifier of the task
:ivar parent_id: the identifier of the parent task, if any
:vartype parent_id: Optional[int]
:ivar str name: the description of the task (if any)
:ivar ~collections.abc.Coroutine coro: the coroutine object of the task

functions.name: anyio.from_thread.run
documentation:
Call a coroutine function from a worker thread.

:param func: a coroutine function
:param args: positional arguments for the callable
:return: the return value of the coroutine function

functions.name: anyio.from_thread.run_sync
documentation:
Call a function in the event loop thread from a worker thread.

:param func: a callable
:param args: positional arguments for the callable
:return: the return value of the callable

functions.name: anyio.from_thread.create_blocking_portal
documentation:
Create a portal for running functions in the event loop thread from external threads.

Use this function in asynchronous code when you need to allow external threads access to the
event loop where your asynchronous code is currently running.

:::{deprecated} 3.0 Use :class:`.BlockingPortal` directly.
:::

functions.name: anyio.from_thread.start_blocking_portal
documentation:
Start a new event loop in a new thread and run a blocking portal in its main task.

The parameters are the same as for {func}`~anyio.run`.

```{eval-rst}

:param backend: name of the backend
:param backend_options: backend options
:return: a context manager that yields a blocking portal
```

:::{versionchanged} 3.0 Usage as a context manager is now required.
:::

classes.name: anyio.from_thread.BlockingPortal

methods.name: anyio.from_thread.BlockingPortal.sleep_until_stopped
documentation: Sleep until {meth}`stop` is called.

methods.name: anyio.from_thread.BlockingPortal.stop
documentation:
Signal the portal to shut down.

This marks the portal as no longer accepting new calls and exits from
{meth}`sleep_until_stopped`.

```{eval-rst}

finish before returning
```

methods.name: anyio.from_thread.BlockingPortal._spawn_task_from_thread
documentation:
Spawn a new task using the given callable.

Implementors must ensure that the future is resolved when the task finishes.

```{eval-rst}

:param func: a callable
:param args: positional arguments to be passed to the callable
:param kwargs: keyword arguments to be passed to the callable
:param name: name of the task (will be coerced to a string if not ``None``)
:param future: a future that will resolve to the return value of the callable, or the
    exception raised during its execution
```

methods.name: anyio.from_thread.BlockingPortal.call
documentation:
Call the given function in the event loop thread.

If the callable returns a coroutine object, it is awaited on.

:param func: any callable
:raises RuntimeError: if the portal is not running or if this method is called from within
    the event loop thread

methods.name: anyio.from_thread.BlockingPortal.spawn_task
documentation:
Start a task in the portal's task group.

```{eval-rst}

:param func: the target coroutine function
:param args: positional arguments passed to ``func``
:param name: name of the task (will be coerced to a string if not ``None``)
:return: a future that resolves with the return value of the callable if the task completes
    successfully, or with the exception raised in the task
:raises RuntimeError: if the portal is not running or if this method is called from within
    the event loop thread
```

:::{versionadded} 2.1
:::

:::{deprecated} 3.0 Use :meth:`start_task_soon` instead. If your code needs AnyIO 2 compatibility, you can keep using this until AnyIO 4.
:::

methods.name: anyio.from_thread.BlockingPortal.start_task_soon
documentation:
Start a task in the portal's task group.

The task will be run inside a cancel scope which can be cancelled by cancelling the
returned future.

```{eval-rst}

:param func: the target coroutine function
:param args: positional arguments passed to ``func``
:param name: name of the task (will be coerced to a string if not ``None``)
:return: a future that resolves with the return value of the callable if the task completes
    successfully, or with the exception raised in the task
:raises RuntimeError: if the portal is not running or if this method is called from within
    the event loop thread
```

:::{versionadded} 3.0
:::

methods.name: anyio.from_thread.BlockingPortal.start_task
documentation:
Start a task in the portal's task group and wait until it signals for readiness.

This method works the same way as {meth}`TaskGroup.start`.

```{eval-rst}

:param func: the target coroutine function
:param args: positional arguments passed to ``func``
:param name: name of the task (will be coerced to a string if not ``None``)
:return: a tuple of (future, task_status_value) where the ``task_status_value`` is the
    value passed to ``task_status.started()`` from within the target function
```

:::{versionadded} 3.0
:::

methods.name: anyio.from_thread.BlockingPortal.wrap_async_context_manager
documentation:
Wrap an async context manager as a synchronous context manager via this portal.

Spawns a task that will call both `__aenter__()` and `__aexit__()`, stopping in the
middle until the synchronous context manager exits.

```{eval-rst}

:param cm: an asynchronous context manager
:return: a synchronous context manager
```

:::{versionadded} 2.1
:::
An object that lets external threads run code in an asynchronous event loop.

functions.name: anyio._core._typedattr.typed_attribute
documentation: Return a unique object, used to mark typed attributes.

classes.name: anyio._core._typedattr.TypedAttributeSet
documentation:
Superclass for typed attribute collections.

Checks that every public attribute of every subclass has a type annotation.

classes.name: anyio._core._typedattr.TypedAttributeProvider

methods.name: anyio._core._typedattr.TypedAttributeProvider.extra_attributes
documentation:
A mapping of the extra attributes to callables that return the corresponding values.

If the provider wraps another provider, the attributes from that wrapper should also be
included in the returned mapping (but the wrapper may override the callables from the
wrapped instance).

methods.name: anyio._core._typedattr.TypedAttributeProvider.extra
documentation:
extra(attribute, default=undefined)

Return the value of the given typed extra attribute.

```{eval-rst}

:param attribute: the attribute (member of a :class:`~TypedAttributeSet`) to look for
:param default: the value that should be returned if no value is found for the attribute
:raises ~anyio.TypedAttributeLookupError: if the search failed and no default value was
    given
```
Base class for classes that wish to provide typed extra attributes.

classes.name: anyio.streams.tls.TLSAttribute
documentation: Contains Transport Layer Security related attributes.

classes.name: anyio.streams.tls.TLSStream

methods.name: anyio.streams.tls.TLSStream.wrap
documentation:
Wrap an existing stream with Transport Layer Security.

This performs a TLS handshake with the peer.

```{eval-rst}

:param transport_stream: a bytes-transporting stream to wrap
:param server_side: ``True`` if this is the server side of the connection, ``False`` if
    this is the client side (if omitted, will be set to ``False`` if ``hostname`` has been
    provided, ``False`` otherwise). Used only to create a default context when an explicit
    context has not been provided.
:param hostname: host name of the peer (if host name checking is desired)
:param ssl_context: the SSLContext object to use (if not provided, a secure default will be
    created)
:param standard_compatible: if ``False``, skip the closing handshake when closing the
    connection, and don't raise an exception if the peer does the same
:raises ~ssl.SSLError: if the TLS handshake fails
```

methods.name: anyio.streams.tls.TLSStream.unwrap
documentation:
Does the TLS closing handshake.

:return: a tuple of (wrapped byte stream, bytes left in the read buffer)

A stream wrapper that encrypts all sent data and decrypts received data.

This class has no public initializer; use {meth}`wrap` instead.
All extra attributes from {class}`~TLSAttribute` are supported.

```{eval-rst}

:var AnyByteStream transport_stream: the wrapped stream
```

classes.name: anyio.streams.tls.TLSListener
documentation:
A convenience listener that wraps another listener and auto-negotiates a TLS session on every
accepted connection.

If the TLS handshake times out or raises an exception, {meth}`handle_handshake_error` is
called to do whatever post-mortem processing is deemed necessary.

Supports only the {attr}`~TLSAttribute.standard_compatible` extra attribute.

```{eval-rst}

:param Listener listener: the listener to wrap
:param ssl_context: the SSL context object
:param standard_compatible: a flag passed through to :meth:`TLSStream.wrap`
:param handshake_timeout: time limit for the TLS handshake
    (passed to :func:`~anyio.fail_after`)
```

functions.name: anyio._core._subprocesses.run_process
documentation:
Run an external command in a subprocess and wait until it completes.

:::{seealso}
{func}`subprocess.run`
:::

```{eval-rst}

executable name or path and its arguments
:param input: bytes passed to the standard input of the subprocess
:param stdout: either :data:`subprocess.PIPE` or :data:`subprocess.DEVNULL`
:param stderr: one of :data:`subprocess.PIPE`, :data:`subprocess.DEVNULL` or
    :data:`subprocess.STDOUT`
:param check: if ``True``, raise :exc:`~subprocess.CalledProcessError` if the process
    terminates with a return code other than 0
:param cwd: If not ``None``, change the working directory to this before running the command
:param env: if not ``None``, this mapping replaces the inherited environment variables from the
    parent process
:param start_new_session: if ``true`` the setsid() system call will be made in the child
    process prior to the execution of the subprocess. (POSIX only)
:return: an object representing the completed process
:raises ~subprocess.CalledProcessError: if ``check`` is ``True`` and the process exits with a
    nonzero return code
```

functions.name: anyio._core._subprocesses.open_process
documentation:
Start an external command in a subprocess.

:::{seealso}
{class}`subprocess.Popen`
:::

```{eval-rst}

executable name or path and its arguments
:param stdin: one of :data:`subprocess.PIPE`, :data:`subprocess.DEVNULL`, a
    file-like object, or ``None``
:param stdout: one of :data:`subprocess.PIPE`, :data:`subprocess.DEVNULL`,
    a file-like object, or ``None``
:param stderr: one of :data:`subprocess.PIPE`, :data:`subprocess.DEVNULL`,
    :data:`subprocess.STDOUT`, a file-like object, or ``None``
:param cwd: If not ``None``, the working directory is changed before executing
:param env: If env is not ``None``, it must be a mapping that defines the environment
    variables for the new process
:param start_new_session: if ``true`` the setsid() system call will be made in the child
    process prior to the execution of the subprocess. (POSIX only)
:return: an asynchronous process object
```

functions.name: anyio.to_process.run_sync
documentation:
Call the given function with the given arguments in a worker process.

If the `cancellable` option is enabled and the task waiting for its completion is cancelled,
the worker process running it will be abruptly terminated using SIGKILL (or
`terminateProcess()` on Windows).

```{eval-rst}

:param func: a callable
:param args: positional arguments for the callable
:param cancellable: ``True`` to allow cancellation of the operation while it's running
:param limiter: capacity limiter to use to limit the total amount of processes running
    (if omitted, the default limiter is used)
:return: an awaitable that yields the return value of the function.
```

functions.name: anyio.to_process.current_default_process_limiter
documentation:
Return the capacity limiter that is used by default to limit the number of worker processes.

:return: a capacity limiter object

functions.name: anyio._core._tasks.open_cancel_scope
documentation:
Open a cancel scope.

```{eval-rst}

:param shield: ``True`` to shield the cancel scope from external cancellation
:return: a cancel scope
```

:::{deprecated} 3.0 Use :class:`~CancelScope` directly.
:::

functions.name: anyio._core._tasks.fail_after
documentation:
Create a context manager which raises a {class}`TimeoutError` if does not finish in time.

```{eval-rst}

disable the timeout
:param shield: ``True`` to shield the cancel scope from external cancellation
:return: a context manager that yields a cancel scope
:rtype: :class:`~typing.ContextManager`\[:class:`~anyio.abc.CancelScope`\]
```

functions.name: anyio._core._tasks.move_on_after
documentation:
Create a cancel scope with a deadline that expires after the given delay.

```{eval-rst}

to disable the timeout
:param shield: ``True`` to shield the cancel scope from external cancellation
:return: a cancel scope
```

functions.name: anyio._core._tasks.current_effective_deadline
documentation:
Return the nearest deadline among all the cancel scopes effective for the current task.

```{eval-rst}

deadline in effect)
:rtype: float
```

functions.name: anyio._core._tasks.create_task_group
documentation:
Create a task group.

:return: a task group

classes.name: anyio._core._tasks.CancelScope

methods.name: anyio._core._tasks.CancelScope.cancel
documentation: Cancel this scope immediately.

methods.name: anyio._core._tasks.CancelScope.deadline
documentation:
The time (clock value) when this scope is cancelled automatically.

Will be `float('inf')` if no timeout has been set.

methods.name: anyio._core._tasks.CancelScope.cancel_called
documentation: `True` if {meth}`cancel` has been called.

methods.name: anyio._core._tasks.CancelScope.shield
documentation:
``True`` if this scope is shielded from external cancellation.

While a scope is shielded, it will not receive cancellations from outside.

Wraps a unit of work that can be made separately cancellable.

```{eval-rst}

:param deadline: The time (clock value) when this scope is cancelled automatically
:param shield: ``True`` to shield the cancel scope from external cancellation
```

functions.name: anyio._core._compat.maybe_async
documentation:
Await on the given object if necessary.

This function is intended to bridge the gap between AnyIO 2.x and 3.x where some functions and
methods were converted from coroutine functions into regular functions.

Do **not** try to use this for any other purpose!

:::{versionadded} 2.2
:::

functions.name: anyio._core._compat.maybe_async_cm
documentation:
Wrap a regular context manager as an async one if necessary.

This function is intended to bridge the gap between AnyIO 2.x and 3.x where some functions and
methods were changed to return regular context managers instead of async ones.

```{eval-rst}

:param cm: a regular or async context manager
:return: an async context manager
```

:::{versionadded} 2.2
:::

functions.name: anyio._core._sockets.connect_tcp
documentation: tls_hostname given

functions.name: anyio._core._sockets.connect_tcp
documentation: ssl_context given

functions.name: anyio._core._sockets.connect_tcp
documentation: tls=True

functions.name: anyio._core._sockets.connect_tcp
documentation: tls=False

functions.name: anyio._core._sockets.connect_tcp
documentation: No TLS arguments

functions.name: anyio._core._sockets.connect_tcp
documentation:
Connect to a host using the TCP protocol.

This function implements the stateless version of the Happy Eyeballs algorithm (RFC 6555).
If `address` is a host name that resolves to multiple IP addresses, each one is tried until
one connection attempt succeeds. If the first attempt does not connected within 250
milliseconds, a second attempt is started using the next address in the list, and so on.
On IPv6 enabled systems, an IPv6 address (if available) is tried first.

When the connection has been established, a TLS handshake will be done if either
`ssl_context` or `tls_hostname` is not `None`, or if `tls` is `True`.

```{eval-rst}

:param remote_host: the IP address or host name to connect to
:param remote_port: port on the target host to connect to
:param local_host: the interface address or name to bind the socket to before connecting
:param tls: ``True`` to do a TLS handshake with the connected stream and return a
    :class:`~anyio.streams.tls.TLSStream` instead
:param ssl_context: the SSL context object to use (if omitted, a default context is created)
:param tls_standard_compatible: If ``True``, performs the TLS shutdown handshake before closing
    the stream and requires that the server does this as well. Otherwise,
    :exc:`~ssl.SSLEOFError` may be raised during reads from the stream.
    Some protocols, such as HTTP, require this option to be ``False``.
    See :meth:`~ssl.SSLContext.wrap_socket` for details.
:param tls_hostname: host name to check the server certificate against (defaults to the value
    of ``remote_host``)
:param happy_eyeballs_delay: delay (in seconds) before starting the next connection attempt
:return: a socket stream object if no TLS handshake was done, otherwise a TLS stream
:raises OSError: if the connection attempt fails
```

functions.name: anyio._core._sockets.connect_unix
documentation:
Connect to the given UNIX socket.

Not available on Windows.

:param path: path to the socket
:return: a socket stream object

functions.name: anyio._core._sockets.create_tcp_listener
documentation:
Create a TCP socket listener.

```{eval-rst}

:param local_port: port number to listen on
:param local_host: IP address of the interface to listen on. If omitted, listen on all IPv4
    and IPv6 interfaces. To listen on all interfaces on a specific address family, use
    ``0.0.0.0`` for IPv4 or ``::`` for IPv6.
:param family: address family (used if ``interface`` was omitted)
:param backlog: maximum number of queued incoming connections (up to a maximum of 2**16, or
    65536)
:param reuse_port: ``True`` to allow multiple sockets to bind to the same address/port
    (not supported on Windows)
:return: a list of listener objects
```

functions.name: anyio._core._sockets.create_unix_listener
documentation:
Create a UNIX socket listener.

Not available on Windows.

```{eval-rst}

:param path: path of the socket
:param mode: permissions to set on the socket
:param backlog: maximum number of queued incoming connections (up to a maximum of 2**16, or
    65536)
:return: a listener object
```

:::{versionchanged} 3.0 If a socket already exists on the file system in the given path, it will be removed first.
:::

functions.name: anyio._core._sockets.create_udp_socket
documentation:
Create a UDP socket.

If `port` has been given, the socket will be bound to this port on the local machine,
making this socket suitable for providing UDP based services.

```{eval-rst}

``local_host`` if omitted
:param local_host: IP address or host name of the local interface to bind to
:param local_port: local port to bind to
:param reuse_port: ``True`` to allow multiple sockets to bind to the same address/port
    (not supported on Windows)
:return: a UDP socket
```

functions.name: anyio._core._sockets.create_connected_udp_socket
documentation:
Create a connected UDP socket.

Connected UDP sockets can only communicate with the specified remote host/port, and any packets
sent from other sources are dropped.

```{eval-rst}

:param remote_host: remote host to set as the default target
:param remote_port: port on the remote host to set as the default target
:param family: address family (``AF_INET`` or ``AF_INET6``) – automatically determined from
    ``local_host`` or ``remote_host`` if omitted
:param local_host: IP address or host name of the local interface to bind to
:param local_port: local port to bind to
:param reuse_port: ``True`` to allow multiple sockets to bind to the same address/port
    (not supported on Windows)
:return: a connected UDP socket
```

functions.name: anyio._core._sockets.getaddrinfo
documentation:
Look up a numeric IP address given a host name.

Internationalized domain names are translated according to the (non-transitional) IDNA 2008
standard.

:::{note}
4-tuple IPv6 socket addresses are automatically converted to 2-tuples of
(host, port), unlike what {func}`socket.getaddrinfo` does.
:::

```{eval-rst}

:param host: host name
:param port: port number
:param family: socket family (`'AF_INET``, ...)
:param type: socket type (``SOCK_STREAM``, ...)
:param proto: protocol number
:param flags: flags to pass to upstream ``getaddrinfo()``
:return: list of tuples containing (family, type, proto, canonname, sockaddr)
```

:::{seealso}
{func}`socket.getaddrinfo`
:::

functions.name: anyio._core._sockets.getnameinfo
documentation:
Look up the host name of an IP address.

```{eval-rst}

:param sockaddr: socket address (e.g. (ipaddress, port) for IPv4)
:param flags: flags to pass to upstream ``getnameinfo()``
:return: a tuple of (host name, service name)
```

:::{seealso}
{func}`socket.getnameinfo`
:::

functions.name: anyio._core._sockets.wait_socket_readable
documentation:
Wait until the given socket has data to be read.

This does **NOT** work on Windows when using the asyncio backend with a proactor event loop
(default on py3.8+).

:::{warning}
Only use this on raw sockets that have not been wrapped by any higher level
constructs like socket streams!
:::

```{eval-rst}

:param sock: a socket object
:raises ~anyio.ClosedResourceError: if the socket was closed while waiting for the
    socket to become readable
:raises ~anyio.BusyResourceError: if another task is already waiting for the socket
    to become readable
```

functions.name: anyio._core._sockets.wait_socket_writable
documentation:
Wait until the given socket can be written to.

This does **NOT** work on Windows when using the asyncio backend with a proactor event loop
(default on py3.8+).

:::{warning}
Only use this on raw sockets that have not been wrapped by any higher level
constructs like socket streams!
:::

```{eval-rst}

:param sock: a socket object
:raises ~anyio.ClosedResourceError: if the socket was closed while waiting for the
    socket to become writable
:raises ~anyio.BusyResourceError: if another task is already waiting for the socket
    to become writable
```

functions.name: anyio._core._sockets.convert_ipv6_sockaddr
documentation:
Convert a 4-tuple IPv6 socket address to a 2-tuple (address, port) format.

If the scope ID is nonzero, it is added to the address, separated with `%`.
Otherwise the flow id and scope id are simply cut off from the tuple.
Any other kinds of socket addresses are returned as-is.

```{eval-rst}

:param sockaddr: the result of :meth:`~socket.socket.getsockname`
:return: the converted socket address
```
Private API

classes.name: anyio._core._synchronization.EventStatistics

classes.name: anyio._core._synchronization.CapacityLimiterStatistics
documentation:
---
ivar float total_tokens: total number of available tokens
ivar int borrowed_tokens: number of tokens currently borrowed by tasks
ivar int tasks_waiting: |-
  number of tasks waiting on {meth}`~.CapacityLimiter.acquire` or

  limiter
---

classes.name: anyio._core._synchronization.LockStatistics
documentation:

ivar int tasks_waiting: number of tasks waiting on {meth}`~.Lock.acquire`
ivar ~anyio.TaskInfo owner: |-
  task currently holding the lock (or `None` if the lock is not
  held by any task)
---

Acquire the underlying lock, without blocking.

:raises ~WouldBlock: if the operation would block

methods.name: anyio._core._synchronization.Semaphore.release
documentation: Increment the semaphore value.

methods.name: anyio._core._synchronization.Semaphore.value
documentation: The current value of the semaphore.

methods.name: anyio._core._synchronization.Semaphore.max_value
documentation: The maximum value of the semaphore.

methods.name: anyio._core._synchronization.Semaphore.statistics
documentation:
Return statistics about the current state of this semaphore.

:::{versionadded} 3.0
:::

methods.name: anyio._core._synchronization.CapacityLimiter.total_tokens
documentation:
The total number of tokens available for borrowing.

This is a read-write property. If the total number of tokens is increased, the
proportionate number of tasks waiting on this limiter will be granted their tokens.

:::{versionchanged} 3.0 The property is now writable.
:::

methods.name: anyio._core._synchronization.CapacityLimiter.borrowed_tokens
documentation: The number of tokens that have currently been borrowed.

methods.name: anyio._core._synchronization.CapacityLimiter.available_tokens
documentation: The number of tokens currently available to be borrowed

methods.name: anyio._core._synchronization.CapacityLimiter.acquire_nowait
documentation:
Acquire a token for the current task without waiting for one to become available.

:raises ~anyio.WouldBlock: if there are no tokens available for borrowing

methods.name: anyio._core._synchronization.CapacityLimiter.acquire_on_behalf_of_nowait
documentation:
Acquire a token without waiting for one to become available.

:param borrower: the entity borrowing a token
:raises ~anyio.WouldBlock: if there are no tokens available for borrowing

methods.name: anyio._core._synchronization.CapacityLimiter.acquire
documentation: Acquire a token for the current task, waiting if necessary for one to become available.

methods.name: anyio._core._synchronization.CapacityLimiter.acquire_on_behalf_of
documentation:
Acquire a token, waiting if necessary for one to become available.

:param borrower: the entity borrowing a token

methods.name: anyio._core._synchronization.CapacityLimiter.release
documentation:
Release the token held by the current task.
:raises RuntimeError: if the current task has not borrowed a token from this limiter.

methods.name: anyio._core._synchronization.CapacityLimiter.release_on_behalf_of
documentation:
Release the token held by the given borrower.

:raises RuntimeError: if the borrower has not borrowed a token from this limiter.

methods.name: anyio._core._synchronization.CapacityLimiter.statistics
documentation:
Return statistics about the current state of this limiter.

:::{versionadded} 3.0
:::

functions.name: anyio._core._fileio.open_file
documentation:
Open a file asynchronously.

The arguments are exactly the same as for the builtin {func}`open`.

```{eval-rst}

:return: an asynchronous file object
```

functions.name: anyio._core._fileio.wrap_file
documentation:
Wrap an existing file as an asynchronous file.

:param file: an existing file-like object
:return: an asynchronous file object

classes.name: anyio._core._fileio.AsyncFile

methods.name: anyio._core._fileio.AsyncFile.wrapped
documentation: The wrapped file object.

An asynchronous file object.

This class wraps a standard file object and provides async friendly versions of the following
blocking methods (where available on the original file object):

- read
- read1
- readline
- readlines
- readinto
- readinto1
- write
- writelines
- truncate
- seek
- tell
- flush

All other methods are directly passed through.

This class supports the asynchronous context manager protocol which closes the underlying file
at the end of the context block.

This class also supports asynchronous iteration:

```
async with await open_file(...) as f:
    async for line in f:
        print(line)
```

classes.name: anyio._core._fileio.Path
documentation:
An asynchronous version of {class}`pathlib.Path`.

This class cannot be substituted for {class}`pathlib.Path` or {class}`pathlib.PurePath`, but
it is compatible with the {class}`os.PathLike` interface.

It implements the Python 3.10 version of {class}`pathlib.Path` interface, except for the
deprecated {meth}`~pathlib.Path.link_to` method.

Any methods that do disk I/O need to be awaited on. These methods are:

- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.absolute`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.chmod`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.cwd`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.exists`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.expanduser`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.group`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.hardlink_to`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.home`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.is_block_device`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.is_char_device`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.is_dir`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.is_fifo`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.is_file`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.is_mount`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.lchmod`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.lstat`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.mkdir`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.open`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.owner`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.read_bytes`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.read_text`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.readlink`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.rename`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.replace`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.rmdir`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.samefile`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.stat`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.touch`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.unlink`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.write_bytes`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.write_text`

Additionally, the following methods return an async iterator yielding {class}`~.Path` objects:

- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.glob`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.iterdir`
- {meth}`~pathlib.Path.rglob`
